key_points: A resource is any material or substance that has utility, value, and can satisfy human needs
Resources become useful only when supported by human knowledge, technology, and skills
Natural resources are unevenly distributed due to variations in climate, geography, and geological structures
Human beings play a key role in resource development through innovation and technological advancement
Sustainable resource management is essential to balance economic development and environmental protection
examples: Soil supporting agriculture and food production
Minerals like iron ore used in manufacturing industries
Hydroelectric power generated from flowing water
Wind turbines producing electricity from wind energy
Recycling metals and plastics to conserve resources
important_terms: Resource Utility: The usefulness of a material that makes it valuable for humans
Human Resource Development: Improving people's skills, education, and health to enhance productivity
Resource Depletion: Reduction of resources due to excessive and unsustainable use
Sustainable Resource Management: Responsible use of resources to ensure long-term availability
Environmental Balance: Maintaining harmony between human activities and the natural environment
exam_tips: Explain the role of technology and human skills in transforming materials into resources
Understand why resources are unevenly distributed across the world
Link resource conservation with sustainable development and environmental protection
Use examples such as renewable energy and recycling to explain conservation methods
Practice explaining how population growth and industrialization affect resource use
